credentials.account: n/a (current compose runtime rebuilt with empty local dataset)

assertions.title: Dealer settlement is guided and explicit

steps.action: Run the milestone-aligned dealer-settlement command and inspect the O2C invariant settlement path.
steps.expected: Dealer settlement should stay header-level, support explicit FIFO/default allocation handling plus FUTURE_APPLICATION carry, and replay without duplicate allocations or journals.
steps.observed: `CR_DealerReceiptSettlementAuditTrailTest` passed inside the 47-test settlement/control run, including `dealerSettlement_isIdempotent_underConcurrency_andRejectsMismatch` and `dealerSettlement_headerLevelFutureApplication_replaysWithoutDuplicateAllocations`. The test proved replay reuses the same journal id, preserves a single invoice-allocation row, and records a second `FUTURE_APPLICATION` row instead of duplicating allocations. `ErpInvariantsSuiteIT` also passed its `/api/v1/accounting/settlements/dealers` path, returning a dealer-settlement journal that stayed balanced and replayed to the same journal id.

tests.result: BUILD SUCCESS; Tests run: 47, Failures: 0, Errors: 0, Skipped: 0

assertions.title: Supplier settlement is guided and replay-safe

steps.action: Run focused supplier-settlement methods plus the P2P invariant golden path.
steps.expected: Supplier settlement should default to oldest-open/header-level allocation, support unapplied carry handling, reject reference-only suppliers, and replay or mismatch fail safely.
steps.observed: The targeted `AccountingServiceTest` command passed 3/3, covering `settleSupplierInvoices_defaultsHeaderLevelOldestOpenAllocationsAndCarriesOnAccountRemainder`, `settleSupplierInvoices_rejectsReferenceOnlySupplierWithExplicitReason`, and `settleSupplierInvoices_replayPayloadMismatchWinsOverNetCashPrevalidation`. Those cases proved oldest-open allocation across two purchases, an explicit `ON_ACCOUNT` remainder row, reference-only supplier denial text, and replay-mismatch fail-closed behavior. `ErpInvariantsSuiteIT` also passed the live `/api/v1/accounting/settlements/suppliers` path and confirmed the replay returned the same supplier-settlement journal id with only one allocation set persisted for idempotency key `P2P-SETTLE-001`.

tests.result: BUILD SUCCESS; Tests run: 3, Failures: 0, Errors: 0, Skipped: 0

crossCheck.result: BUILD SUCCESS; Tests run: 47, Failures: 0, Errors: 0, Skipped: 0

assertions.title: Manual journals are controlled adjustments only

steps.action: Run focused manual-journal validation and audit-metadata tests, then confirm replay-safe manual posting behavior.
steps.expected: Manual journals should reject missing reasons, carry attachment/audit metadata through the posting surface, and remain explicit `MANUAL` adjustments instead of hidden workflow glue.
steps.observed: The targeted 2-test command passed: `AccountingServiceStandardJournalTest#createManualJournal_requiresReason` rejected blank reason text with `Manual journal reason is required`, and `AccountingComplianceAuditServiceTest#recordJournalCreation_manualJournal_flagsSensitiveOperation` recorded `MANUAL_JOURNAL_CREATED` with `sensitiveOperation=true`, `journalSource=MANUAL`, and before/after state metadata. The broader settlement/control run also passed `CR_ManualJournalSafetyTest`, whose concurrency and retry cases proved manual journals are persisted as `JournalEntryType.MANUAL` / `sourceModule=MANUAL`, remain balanced, and do not leave orphan mappings on retry.

tests.result: BUILD SUCCESS; Tests run: 2, Failures: 0, Errors: 0, Skipped: 0

crossCheck.result: BUILD SUCCESS; Tests run: 47, Failures: 0, Errors: 0, Skipped: 0

assertions.title: Closed-period posting requires a timed admin exception

steps.action: Run the policy test suite for maker-checker close control and inspect the closed-period exception service.
steps.expected: Direct period close should be blocked in favor of maker-checker approval, approvals should require admin authority and reason, and closed-period posting exceptions should be per-document, mandatory-reason, one-hour grants.
steps.observed: `AccountingPeriodServicePolicyTest` passed all 14 policy cases inside the 47-test run. `closePeriod_requiresApprovedMakerCheckerRequestForOpenPeriod` and the locked-period variant both rejected direct close with `submit /request-close and approve`, `approvePeriodClose_allowsLockedToClosedWhenReasonProvided` completed the approved path, `approvePeriodClose_requiresAdminRole` rejected non-admin approval, and `reopenPeriod_requiresReasonForClosedPeriod` enforced reason capture. The current closed-period exception service ties authorization to admin or super-admin actors, normalizes `documentType`, `documentReference`, and `reason`, and persists `expiresAt = now + 1 hour` per document.

tests.result: BUILD SUCCESS; Tests run: 47, Failures: 0, Errors: 0, Skipped: 0

assertions.title: Business exceptions are admin-only, per-document, and auditable

steps.action: Run the settlement-override policy tests and inspect the immutable audit metadata behavior.
steps.expected: Out-of-policy settlement overrides should fail closed without approval, require the correct reason code and stable audit metadata, and only proceed when a scoped approval is present.
steps.observed: `InvoiceSettlementPolicyTest` passed all 11 cases in the settlement/control run. The suite proved `settlementOverrideFailsClosedWhenApprovalIsMissing`, `settlementOverrideRequiresOverrideReasonCode`, `settlementOverrideAppliesWhenApproved`, and `settlementApprovalMetadataRequiresSourceKey`. These checks required a `SETTLEMENT_OVERRIDE` approval record, rejected blank reference/metadata, and preserved immutable audit metadata for the approved business exception.

tests.result: BUILD SUCCESS; Tests run: 47, Failures: 0, Errors: 0, Skipped: 0

frictions.description: Launching the dedicated `user-testing-flow-validator` Task droid failed immediately with `Invalid model: custom:CLIProxyAPI-5.4-high-pro`.
frictions.resolution: Completed the assigned settlement/control validation directly in-session and wrote the flow artifact manually, per mission guidance for helper-launch failures.

frictions.description: The rebuilt compose-backed runtime came up with an empty local dataset (`companies=0`, `app_users=0`), so no seeded tenant/user journey was available for live settlement execution on `http://localhost:8081`.
frictions.resolution: Used the runtime only as a reachability probe (`/api/v1/auth/me` returned 403 while `9090/actuator/health` stayed 503/DOWN) and pivoted to deterministic Spring Boot API/e2e suites for assertion evidence.

frictions.description: Exploratory broad suites `SettlementE2ETest`, `PeriodCloseLockIT`, and `CR_PurchasingToApAccountingTest` still encode pre-milestone assumptions and failed against the current contracts (for example direct-close instead of maker-checker, and older purchase-receipt setup expectations).
frictions.resolution: Recorded the preferred milestone-aligned suites in `.factory/library/user-testing.md` and based the validation result on the passing targeted settlement/control commands above.

summary: Validated the five settlement/control assertions for `corrections-and-control` using the real API surface where available and deterministic Spring Boot coverage where the rebuilt compose runtime lacked seeded data. The passing commands proved manual-journal reason/audit enforcement, admin-scoped override approval, maker-checker close control, replay-safe dealer and supplier settlements, and per-document one-hour closed-period exception wiring, while exploratory legacy suites were documented as stale guidance rather than milestone blockers.
